summary: Iteration 30: Retest of FDI Dental Chart centering fix in Add Implant Position modal. FAIL - The fix (adding style={{ flex: 1 }} to ScrollView) was applied but centering is NOT working. The contentContainerStyle with justifyContent: 'center' is not being rendered in the DOM on React Native Web.

ui_bugs.issue: Dental chart content is NOT vertically centered. Content appears at TOP with ~776px empty space below. The style={{ flex: 1 }} was added to ScrollView but contentContainerStyle justifyContent: center is NOT being applied in the DOM (shows 'normal' instead of 'center').
ui_bugs.fix_required: React Native Web doesn't render contentContainerStyle the same as native. Need to wrap content in a View with flex:1 and justifyContent:center, or use a different approach for web centering.

action_items: FIX REQUIRED: The contentContainerStyle with justifyContent:'center' is NOT being applied by React Native Web. Current code at line 331: <ScrollView style={{ flex: 1 }} contentContainerStyle={ms.scroll}> with ms.scroll = { padding: 16, flexGrow: 1, justifyContent: 'center' } - the justifyContent:center is not rendering in browser DOM.

ALTERNATIVE FIX: Wrap the step content (View at line 334) with a flex:1 container that has justifyContent:center, e.g.: <View style={{ flex: 1, justifyContent: 'center' }}><View>...step content...</View></View>
OR: Use a native web approach with CSS - the inner content container needs to expand to fill the scroll area
critical_code_review_comments: React Native Web's ScrollView contentContainerStyle doesn't work the same as native - justifyContent:center is not being applied
The DOM analysis shows flexGrow:1 IS applied to ScrollView (level 3) but justifyContent remains 'normal' at all levels
context_for_next_testing_agent: The style={{ flex: 1 }} fix was applied to ScrollView but centering still not working. DOM analysis shows: Level 3 (ScrollView) has flexGrow:1 but Level 2 (content container) has justifyContent:normal instead of center. React Native Web doesn't properly apply contentContainerStyle justifyContent. Need alternative approach - likely wrapping the View inside ScrollView with flex:1 + justifyContent:center.

UI_Fix_2_Dental_Chart_Centering.description: Dental chart in Add Implant modal is NOT vertically centered - appears at TOP with large empty space below
UI_Fix_2_Dental_Chart_Centering.root_cause: React Native Web's ScrollView doesn't apply contentContainerStyle justifyContent in the same way as native. The style={{ flex: 1 }} on ScrollView was applied correctly, but contentContainerStyle with justifyContent:'center' is not rendering.
UI_Fix_2_Dental_Chart_Centering.fix_suggestion: Wrap content in View with flex:1 and justifyContent:center, or add minHeight:'100%' to contentContainerStyle to force it to fill the scroll area

flows_tested: Analyze modal content positioning with DOM inspection
screenshots: Modal showing content stuck at top with empty space below

rca_of_the_issue.problem: Content not vertically centered in Add Implant Position modal
rca_of_the_issue.applied_fix: Added style={{ flex: 1 }} to ScrollView at line 331
rca_of_the_issue.why_fix_didnt_work: React Native Web renders ScrollView differently than native. The contentContainerStyle prop with justifyContent:'center' is not being applied to the DOM. Browser shows justifyContent:normal on all parent elements.
reproduction_steps: 3. Open any procedure with implant plan
5. Observe: Content at top, large empty space below
rca_of_the_issue.recommended_fix: Change approach - wrap the inner View content with a flex container: <ScrollView style={{ flex: 1 }}><View style={{ flex: 1, justifyContent: 'center', minHeight: '100%' }}><View>...step content...</View></View></ScrollView>
